comment.body:
absurdity is when you arrive to the border of our system. it is across the logical singularity. it is when you realize that inifnity and nothing are the same concepts, that lies outside of our system. 
it is when you realize that every system that assigns meanings to each known elements of the world, is nothing but the projection of our neural web.
that's absurdity.
and this was the enlightment of the 20th century that gave birth for true avantgarde (Dali, Pollock, Schönberg, Monthy Python...)

comment.body: It has been a while since I did not think about it. I remember I met a girl one night, she was super interesting. We literally talked for 5/6 hrs in a club , outside. I was fascinated, she was answering questions I was never been able to find (maybe because i did not really look into it). But about life, the meaningful of it, etc. She was kept asking me if I had a question of her, a specific one. But at the time I did not understand

comment.body:
i went through the religions, but ultimately what i found is that the saints in every religion and every era, including the greeks, have always had one thing in common: the socratesian conclusion, the "Docta Ignoratia"
Nicolaus Cusanus even wrote a book with that title. he was one of the most famous mathematician and a priest (bishop level)
beside Socrates, I think thats the only book, worth to read first, and digest it
